title: Cost of Capital: If a company has a current stock price of 100...
summary: Calculate or evaluate the cost of capital using appropriate financial models.
prompt: If a company has a current stock price of $100 and is expected to pay a dividend of $4 next year, what is the expected growth rate if the required rate of return is 12%?
solution: Rearranging the Gordon Growth Model: g = k - (D1 / P0) = 0.12 - ($4 / $100) = 0.12 - 0.04 = 0.08 or 8%. This expected growth rate indicates how fast the company is anticipated to grow in terms of dividends.
